hero.title: Ship AI, automation, SaaS, and Shopify—without the overhead.
hero.description: We help founders, operators, and growing teams move faster: AI features, workflow automation, product builds, Shopify improvements, and urgent fixes.
hero.trustLine: Trusted by startups, growing companies, and established brands

whyChoose.title: Focused delivery, proven execution, realistic support
whyChoose.description: We deliver working software, not vague research. Clear scope, short phases, and measurable outcomes—every time.

points.title: Focused delivery
points.description: We scope projects clearly, work in short phases, and hand off working software—not vague research or endless revisions.

points.title: Proven execution
points.description: 18 delivered projects, 42 automated workflows, 98% client satisfaction. We know what works in real production environments.

points.title: Realistic support
points.description: Response expectations are set upfront. Support paths are documented. Changes are confirmed in writing. No surprises.

points.title: Clear communication
points.description: Weekly updates, open roadmap conversations, and real progress photos—so you always know what's happening.

services.name: Quick Fixes & Support
services.summary: Small but important website, Shopify, automation, and product tasks for teams that need fast progress without a large engagement.
services.bestFor: Founders and small teams who need a bug fixed, a form repaired, a store updated, or a workflow cleaned up quickly.
services.pain: Important fixes and small improvements keep getting delayed because they feel too small for a full project.
services.outcomes: A clearly scoped fix, cleaner user experience, and faster momentum without turning a simple job into a heavy build.
services.nextStep: Send the issue, current setup, and deadline. We'll confirm whether it fits a quick support sprint or needs a larger scope.

governance.evidenceSummary: Practical entry point for small projects and urgent fixes.

services.name: AI Features & Automation
services.summary: Practical AI work such as assistants, recommendations, document processing, forecasting, and internal automation.
services.bestFor: Teams that want a useful AI feature or internal workflow improvement without commissioning a vague research project.
services.pain: Teams want AI results but do not need a large, expensive build before they know what will work.
services.outcomes: Useful AI features that reduce manual work, speed up responses, and fit into real business workflows.
services.nextStep: Share the task, data source, and where the AI output should appear. We'll suggest a pilot or delivery sprint.

governance.evidenceSummary: Good fit for pilots, internal tools, and phased AI rollouts that need early proof of value.

services.name: Computer Vision & OCR
services.summary: Vision systems for OCR, inspection, detection, image sorting, and document workflows that are too manual today.
services.bestFor: Businesses dealing with invoices, labels, inspections, screenshots, photos, or other image-heavy workflows.
services.pain: Teams still review images, labels, invoices, or media files manually, which slows output and increases mistakes.
services.outcomes: Faster processing, better consistency, and a practical review flow for edge cases.
services.nextStep: Show us a few sample files or camera inputs and the decision you need the system to make.

governance.evidenceSummary: Often starts as a pilot on one document or camera before scaling into larger production systems.

services.name: Workflow Automation
services.summary: Practical automation for leads, reporting, approvals, admin work, and repetitive tasks that waste team time.
services.bestFor: Operations teams and small businesses losing hours to repetitive admin across email, sheets, forms, CRMs, or approvals.
services.pain: Staff still copy data by hand, chase approvals, and repeat the same tasks across tools.
services.outcomes: Less manual work, faster turnaround, and simpler operations your team can actually maintain.
services.nextStep: Tell us which steps are manual today and which tools are involved. We'll identify quick wins and the right rollout size.

governance.evidenceSummary: Strong fit for local businesses and growing teams seeking measurable savings without building a full platform.

services.name: Shopify Storefront & Support
services.summary: Store fixes, conversion improvements, app integrations, and day-to-day Shopify engineering for growing brands.
services.bestFor: Brands that need storefront fixes, conversion improvements, integrations, or ongoing Shopify technical help.
services.pain: Stores lose sales because of theme issues, slow updates, and disconnected operational workflows.
services.outcomes: Cleaner storefront performance, smoother operations, and focused improvements that support sales.
services.nextStep: Share your store URL, the issue, and the result you want. We'll tell you whether it fits a quick fix or a focused sprint.

governance.evidenceSummary: Can start with a small fix, a conversion sprint, or a larger store improvement plan.

services.name: SaaS MVP & Product Builds
services.summary: From MVPs and client portals to internal tools and full web products, built with clear phases and realistic scope.
services.bestFor: Founders and teams planning an MVP, client portal, internal tool, or the next phase of an existing product.
services.pain: Teams have product ideas but get stuck between no-code limits, unclear scope, and expensive agency proposals.
services.outcomes: A usable product, cleaner roadmap, and a delivery plan that fits your current stage and budget.
services.nextStep: Send the product idea, must-have features, and timeline. We'll recommend a discovery sprint, MVP plan, or build phase.

governance.evidenceSummary: Can begin as a paid discovery sprint, prototype, or lean MVP before expanding into a larger phase.

caseStudies.title: Structured data extraction for a content research platform
caseStudies.summary: Aixploria needed structured weekly data without the hours of manual cleanup that were slowing delivery.
caseStudies.before: Researchers were spending too much time extracting records, fixing formatting, and reviewing inconsistent outputs.
caseStudies.after: We built a structured extraction workflow with validation and scheduled exports, turning a manual weekly task into a repeatable process.
timeline: Week 1: review sources and define the output schema
Week 2: build the crawler, parser, and validation rules
Week 3: QA the workflow, schedule delivery, and hand over the process
evidence: Delivery focused on one repeatable weekly workflow instead of a broad platform rebuild.
Validation rules added trust before export.
Handoff made the workflow usable without daily engineering support.

governance.evidenceSummary: Reflects a scoped delivery with documented workflow change and measurable weekly time savings.

caseStudies.title: Forecasting workflow for a research team
caseStudies.summary: A research team needed a better way to test scenarios and produce outputs without relying on fragmented manual analysis.
caseStudies.before: Historical data, model testing, and reporting were split across disconnected files and ad hoc processes.
caseStudies.after: We built a forecasting workflow with cleaner data prep, model evaluation, and dashboard outputs for faster scenario testing.
timeline: Week 1: clean data sources and define the forecasting pipeline
Week 2: build baseline models and evaluation steps
Week 3: optimize outputs and package the reporting workflow
evidence: Work centered on a measurable decision workflow rather than a one-off model experiment.
Scenario testing and reporting brought into one cleaner delivery path.
Reporting layer reduced time from raw inputs to decision-ready outputs.

governance.evidenceSummary: Captures a practical forecasting workflow with documented model improvements and reporting time savings.

trustItems.title: Security & access
trustItems.summary: Projects are built with secure defaults, limited access, and review before release.
commitments: Secrets stay out of source control and shared documents.
Production access is limited to the people who need it.
Sensitive changes are reviewed before release.
evidence: The backend uses Helmet, CORS, rate limiting, request IDs, and input validation.
Access expectations are documented during delivery.

governance.evidenceSummary: Security statements stay tied to real controls and are reviewed against current delivery setup before publication.

trustItems.title: Delivery process
trustItems.summary: You get a clear path from discovery to handoff, with scope, milestones, and visible progress.
commitments: We align on scope before build work starts.
Implementation is broken into clear delivery phases.
QA, release, and handoff are part of the plan, not an afterthought.
evidence: Public service entries describe scope, deliverables, and timelines.
Case studies show the before, approach, and outcome instead of vague claims.
Website content is served from a structured backend source.

governance.evidenceSummary: Process language is reviewed against the actual delivery model so the site matches how projects are run.

trustItems.title: Data handling
trustItems.summary: Project data, credentials, and lead information are handled with clear boundaries and secure transport.
commitments: Lead submissions are validated before persistence.
Policy-sensitive statements stay aligned with public legal pages.
evidence: Lead intake is validated through shared schemas before persistence.
Privacy and cookie policy pages are published with contact routes.
Request metadata capture is explicit in the backend lead flow.

governance.evidenceSummary: Data-handling statements remain aligned with the live intake flow, storage configuration, and public policy pages.

trustItems.title: Communication & support
trustItems.summary: Clients should know what is in progress, what is blocked, and what happens next.
commitments: Response expectations are set early and stated conservatively.
Support and change requests are confirmed in writing.
Pre-sales and policy contact paths remain public and monitored.
evidence: The website publishes contact paths, booking flow, and reply guidance.
Terms avoid implying guaranteed acceptance without a written agreement.
Support statements are reviewed from the published content record.

governance.evidenceSummary: Communication and support statements are reviewed against current staffing, public contact routes, and written agreements.

qualificationQuestions: What do you need help building or improving?
What business problem should this solve?
Who will use it and how often?
Which tools, platforms, or systems need to connect?

siteConfig.title: Automivex | AI, Automation, SaaS & Shopify Engineering
siteConfig.description: Automivex helps founders, operators, and growing teams ship AI features, workflow automation, SaaS products, Shopify improvements, and urgent software fixes.

organizationStructuredData.name: Automivex
